title: Pumpkin Matar
subtitle: with Coconut Yogurt and Brown Basmati Rice
description: Bright tomato and creamy coconut milk deliver a delicious base for this seriously satisfying dinner. You'll toss tender roasted pumpkin into the sauce for an extra layer of flavor, and add some texture with a sprinkle of pumpkin seeds.

steps.title: COOK THE RICE
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Add brown basmati rice, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cover. Cook until all water is absorbed and rice is tender, about 30 to 35 minutes.

steps.title: ROAST THE PUMPKIN
steps.text: Add the cubed pumpkin to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and roast until tender and browned in places, about 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: PREPARE THE PRODUCE
steps.text: Peel and chop the onion. Peel and mince or grate 1 clove garlic. Peel and mince or grate the ginger. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: ADD THE AROMATICS
steps.text: Add the garlic and ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the garam masala and toast for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.

steps.title: ADD THE PUMPKIN
steps.text: Add the tomato paste, coconut milk, and ½ cup water to the skillet, and lower heat to medium. Simmer sauce until slightly thickened, about 4 to 6 minutes. Add the roasted pumpkin and green peas to the simmering sauce and season with salt and pepper. Stir to combine.

steps.title: SERVE IT UP
steps.text: Divide brown basmati rice between large plates or bowls and top with the pumpkin matar. Garnish with pumpkin seeds and yogurt. Enjoy!

tips: TIP: Use a microplane to grate the garlic and ginger if you have one.
TIP: Watch the garam masala carefully so it does not burn.
Once you add the coconut milk, keep a sauce at a simmer to keep it smooth.
*You'll use the head of garlic in this recipe bag in all recipes this week.
